title: Design Tokens: From Figma Variables to CSS Custom Properties
content:
Why tokens matter

Design tokens are the single source of truth that connects the decisions made in Figma with the values rendered in production. A token like color.brand.primary carries meaning — it says 'this is our primary brand colour' — rather than encoding a specific hex value that could change over time.

Our token pipeline

In the Green Design System, tokens originate as Figma variables. A custom export plugin transforms them into a structured JSON format, which is then processed by our token build pipeline into CSS custom properties, TypeScript constants, and Tailwind configuration values.

Semantic vs. primitive

We distinguish between primitive tokens (raw values like #0092E1) and semantic tokens (purpose-driven references like --gds-color-brand-01). Components always consume semantic tokens, never primitives. This ensures that a full theme switch can be achieved by swapping a single token set at the root level.

Dark mode

Green supports both light and dark themes through the gds-theme component. Wrapping any subtree in <gds-theme color-scheme="dark"> automatically inverts all semantic colour tokens to their dark-mode counterparts.
